entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The primary cereal crop studied for yield improvements in a mono-cropping system.

entities.name: LEGUME CROPS
entities.description: Crops used in rotation and as residues to improve the yield of pearl millet.

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The overall productivity of the crop, including grain and dry residue.

entities.name: ROCK PHOSPHATE
entities.description: A phosphorus-containing mineral applied to the soil to assess its effect on crop yield.

entities.name: SANDY ACID SOIL
entities.description: The specific soil type and condition where the experiment was conducted.

entities.name: ICRISAT SAHELIAN CENTER
entities.description: The research facility in Sadore where the study was performed.

entities.name: NIGER
entities.description: The country where the research was conducted.

entities.name: CROP ROTATION
entities.description: The practice of rotating legume crops with pearl millet to improve sustainability and yield.

entities.name: MONO-CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing a single crop species, used as a baseline for comparison.

entities.name: UREA
entities.description: A nitrogen-rich fertilizer applied in micro-doses to improve crop yield.

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The specific measure of grain production per unit area.

entities.name: DRY RESIDUE YIELD
entities.description: The specific measure of dry plant material remaining after harvest.

entities.name: GROWTH PARAMETERS
entities.description: Measurable characteristics used to assess the growth of pearl millet.

entities.name: SOIL TILLAGE
entities.description: The mechanical manipulation of soil, mentioned as being kept to a minimum in the experiment.
